zh.Of Hanmer,Flintshire,England: 英國威爾斯福林爾韓麥
zh.Cardington, Bedfordshire, England, UK: 英國英格蘭貝德福爾卡丁頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Kynaston or Knutson於1547年7月10日出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾米德爾時，他的父親Roger Kynaston or Knutson27歲，母親Maria Hanmer26歲。他於1541年2月7日與Margeriae在英國英格蘭史洛普爾米德爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世在英國英格蘭史洛普爾。

zh-hans.Cardington, Bedfordshire, England, UK: 英国英格兰貝德福爾卡丁頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Kynaston or Knutson于1547年7月10日出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾米德爾时，他的父亲Roger Kynaston or Knutson27岁，母亲Maria Hanmer26岁。他于1541年2月7日与Margeriae在英国英格兰史洛普爾米德爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世在英国英格兰史洛普爾。

nameMeanings.name: Kynaston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Kynaston: in Kinnerley (Shropshire), in Hentland (Herefordshire), or in Much Marcle (Herefordshire).

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Knutson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Knute, Selmer, Ingard, Jarl, Jorgen, Juel, Nels, Obert, Tryg, Algot, Egil.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Knutsson: patronymic from the personal name Knut (see Knudsen ).
Americanized form of Norwegian   Knutsen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Knuteson
meanings: Variant of   Knutson  , a surname of Swedish or Norwegian origin.

variants.name: Knudsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Knud, Niels, Holger, Jorgen, Ove, Alf, Alfhild, Anders, Baard, Berger, Einer.
Danish, Norwegian, and North German: patronymic from the Danish personal name Knud, derived from Old Norse Knútr meaning ‘knot’. Knud was a Danish royal name, Latinized as Canutus.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Kynaston or Knutson was born on 10 July 1547, in Myddle, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Roger Kynaston or Knutson, was 27 and his mother, Maria Hanmer, was 26. He married Margeriae on 7 February 1541, in Myddle, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Of Hanmer,Flintshire,England: Hanmer, Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Kynaston or Knutson est né le 10 juillet 1547, à Myddle, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger Kynaston or Knutson, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Maria Hanmer, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Margeriae le 7 février 1541, à Myddle, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Februar 1541 in Myddle, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Margeriae geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Kynaston or Knutson è nato il il 10 luglio 1547 nel Myddle, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Roger Kynaston or Knutson, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Maria Hanmer, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Margeriae il 7 febbraio 1541, nel Myddle, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Kynaston or Knutsonが1547年7月10日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアムイッドルで生まれた時，父Roger Kynaston or Knutsonは27歳，母Maria Hanmerは26歳であった。1541年2月7日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアムイッドルでMargeriaeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Kynaston or Knutson(이)가 1547년 7월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 마이들일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Kynaston or Knutson(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria Hanmer(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1541년 2월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 마이들일에서 Margeriae(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Kynaston or Knutson nasceu em 10 de julho de 1547, em Myddle, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger Kynaston or Knutson, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Maria Hanmer, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Margeriae em 7 de fevereiro de 1541, em Myddle, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Kynaston or Knutson родился 10 июля 1547 г. в местности миддл, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger Kynaston or Knutson было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria Hanmer, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Margeriae 7 февраля 1541 г. в местности миддл, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Kynaston or Knutson nació el 10 de julio de 1547, en Myddle, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger Kynaston or Knutson, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Maria Hanmer, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Margeriae el 7 de febrero de 1541, en Myddle, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
